exit_criteria: User can message the bot and receive a response from Claude within 8 seconds
Bot silently drops messages from any chat_id not matching TELEGRAM_ALLOWED_CHAT_ID
User can ask 'what have I got today?' and receive a formatted list of calendar events
User can ask about events on a specific date or range
Conversation context persists across messages (rolling 20-message window)
Bot sends typing indicator while agent is processing
User can create a calendar event from natural language with confirmation
User can update an existing calendar event with confirmation
User can delete a calendar event with confirmation
User can ask if they are free at a given time

tasks.title: Repository and monorepo scaffold
tasks.description: Initialise pnpm workspace with packages/bot, packages/orchestrator, packages/shared. Configure TypeScript strict mode for all packages. Add root tsconfig.json with project references. Add .env.example documenting all required environment variables.
acceptance: pnpm install completes without errors
tsc --noEmit passes for all packages
.env.example lists TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN, TELEGRAM_ALLOWED_CHAT_ID, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, DATABASE_URL, DIGEST_CRON, TZ

tasks.title: Shared: db, logger, env, types
tasks.description: Implement packages/shared/src/db.ts (pg Pool singleton), packages/shared/src/logger.ts (pino structured logger), packages/shared/src/env.ts (validated env config using a schema — throws on missing vars at startup), packages/shared/src/types.ts (shared TypeScript interfaces: ConversationMessage, ConfirmationPayload, etc.).
acceptance: db.ts exports a Pool instance; Pool is created once and reused
env.ts throws a descriptive error if any required variable is missing at startup
logger.ts exports a pino logger with level configurable via LOG_LEVEL env var
types.ts exports ConversationMessage and ConfirmationPayload interfaces

tasks.title: Database migrations runner
tasks.description: Implement a migrations runner script at packages/shared/src/migrate.ts. On startup, creates the migrations table if absent, then applies any unapplied SQL migration files from a db/migrations/ directory in order. Migration filenames are zero-padded integers (e.g. 0001_init.sql). Applied migrations are recorded in the migrations table and never re-run.
acceptance: Running migrate.ts twice applies migrations exactly once
0001_init.sql creates conversation_context table with correct schema and index
Migration failures log the error and exit with code 1

tasks.title: conversation_context schema migration
tasks.description: Write db/migrations/0001_init.sql. Creates conversation_context table with id, chat_id, role (CHECK: user|assistant), content, created_at, and an active_confirmation JSONB column (nullable) on the row keyed by chat_id for the confirmation pattern. Adds the index on (chat_id, created_at DESC). Also creates the migrations table itself if not handled by the runner.
acceptance: Migration runs cleanly against a fresh PostgreSQL 16 database
conversation_context table has all columns specified in architecture.md
Index idx_conversation_context_chat_id_created_at is created
active_confirmation JSONB column is present and nullable

tasks.title: Bot service: entrypoint and message handler
tasks.description: Implement packages/bot/src/index.ts. Initialise node-telegram-bot-api in webhook or polling mode (configurable via BOT_MODE env var). Register onText('/.*/') handler that forwards the message payload to the orchestrator service over an internal HTTP call (ORCHESTRATOR_URL env var). Register callback_query handler that forwards callback data. Handle errors gracefully — log and send a plain 'Something went wrong' reply so the user is never left hanging.
acceptance: Bot starts without error when TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN is set
Incoming messages are forwarded to ORCHESTRATOR_URL via POST /message
Callback queries are forwarded to ORCHESTRATOR_URL via POST /callback
Network errors to orchestrator are caught; user receives error message

tasks.title: Bot service: chat_id whitelist middleware
tasks.description: Implement packages/bot/src/middleware.ts. Export a function isAllowedChat(chatId: number): boolean that compares against TELEGRAM_ALLOWED_CHAT_ID. In index.ts, call this before forwarding to orchestrator. Unauthorised messages are silently dropped and logged at WARN level with the offending chat_id. No reply is sent to the unauthorised sender.
acceptance: Message from TELEGRAM_ALLOWED_CHAT_ID is forwarded to orchestrator
Message from any other chat_id is dropped with no reply
A WARN log entry is written containing the unauthorised chat_id
Unit test: isAllowedChat returns true for allowed ID, false for any other

tasks.title: Bot service: inline keyboard builder
tasks.description: Implement packages/bot/src/keyboard.ts. Export buildConfirmKeyboard(): InlineKeyboardMarkup that returns a three-button inline keyboard: Confirm (callback_data: 'confirm'), Edit (callback_data: 'edit'), Cancel (callback_data: 'cancel'). Export buildDismissKeyboard(nudgeId: number): InlineKeyboardMarkup for nudge dismiss buttons.
acceptance: buildConfirmKeyboard returns valid InlineKeyboardMarkup with three buttons
buildDismissKeyboard returns InlineKeyboardMarkup with a single Dismiss button
callback_data values are exactly 'confirm', 'edit', 'cancel', 'dismiss:<nudgeId>'

tasks.title: Orchestrator service: entrypoint and HTTP server
tasks.description: Implement packages/orchestrator/src/index.ts. Start an HTTP server (Node http module, no framework). Handle POST /message: parse body, call agent, return reply text. Handle POST /callback: parse callback data, route to confirm/edit/cancel handler. Run migrations on startup before the server begins accepting requests. Log service start and port.
acceptance: POST /message with valid body returns 200 and a text reply
POST /callback with callback_data 'cancel' returns 200
Migrations run before first request is handled
Server listens on PORT env var, defaults to 3001

tasks.title: Orchestrator: conversation context persistence
tasks.description: Implement context load and save in packages/orchestrator/src/agent.ts (or a dedicated context.ts). loadContext(chatId): fetches the last 20 messages from conversation_context ordered by created_at DESC, returns them oldest-first for the API messages array. saveMessage(chatId, role, content): inserts a row, then deletes any rows beyond the 20-message rolling window for that chat_id. All queries are parameterised.
acceptance: loadContext returns messages in chronological order (oldest first)
After 21st message is saved, only 20 rows remain for that chat_id
All SQL uses parameterised queries — no string interpolation
Unit test: saving 25 messages leaves exactly 20 in DB

tasks.title: Orchestrator: agent core — Anthropic API client and tool loop
tasks.description: Implement the agent loop in packages/orchestrator/src/agent.ts. Assemble system prompt with five blocks in order: Identity, Live context (current datetime + TZ), People index (empty in Phase 1), Pending nudges (empty in Phase 1), Active automations (empty in Phase 1). Append conversation history as messages array. Call claude-sonnet-4-20250514 with tool definitions. Implement tool loop: if response contains tool_use blocks, execute the tool, append tool_result, call API again. Return final text response. P95 latency target: 8 seconds.
acceptance: Agent returns a text response for a plain 'hello' message
Tool loop executes tools and feeds results back until no more tool_use blocks
System prompt contains all five blocks in correct order
Model ID is claude-sonnet-4-20250514 — not hardcoded elsewhere

tasks.title: Bot: typing indicator
tasks.description: In the orchestrator's /message handler (or the bot's forwarding logic), send sendChatAction 'typing' to the bot service before invoking the agent. The bot service must expose a POST /typing endpoint (or the orchestrator calls the Telegram API directly if bot token is available). Typing indicator must be sent before the agent call and should not block the agent response.
acceptance: Telegram shows 'typing...' before the agent response arrives
Typing action is sent before the Anthropic API call is initiated
Failure to send typing indicator does not prevent the agent from responding

tasks.title: Calendar tool wrappers: read operations
tasks.description: Implement packages/orchestrator/src/tools/calendar.ts. Wrap Google Calendar MCP tools: get_todays_events (no params) and get_events_range (start: ISO 8601, end: ISO 8601). Export TypeScript functions that can be called by the agent tool loop. Export the corresponding Anthropic tool definition objects (name, description, input_schema) so they can be included in the API call.
acceptance: get_todays_events tool definition matches MCP contract
get_events_range accepts start and end as ISO 8601 strings
Tool definitions are exported and included in agent API call
Empty calendar response returns a graceful 'No events' message to user

tasks.title: EP-01-03: 'What have I got today?' intent
tasks.description: Wire get_todays_events into the agent loop so that when the user asks about today's events, the agent calls the tool and formats the result as a readable list. Handle empty calendar gracefully ('You have nothing scheduled today.'). Events should be listed chronologically with start time, title, and location if present.
acceptance: Smoke test 3: 'what have I got today?' returns a formatted event list
Empty calendar returns a clear empty-state message
Events are in chronological order with time and title

tasks.title: EP-01-04: Events on a specific date or range
tasks.description: Wire get_events_range into the agent loop. Agent must resolve natural language date references ('next Tuesday', 'this week', 'tomorrow') to ISO 8601 before calling the tool. Live context block in system prompt provides current datetime and TZ to enable this resolution. Handle multi-day ranges and single-day queries.
acceptance: 'What have I got next Tuesday?' calls get_events_range with correct ISO dates
'What's on this week?' calls get_events_range from Monday to Sunday of current week
Natural language date resolution uses TZ from env, not UTC

tasks.title: Calendar tool wrappers: write operations
tasks.description: Extend packages/orchestrator/src/tools/calendar.ts with write tool wrappers: create_event (title, start, end, location?, description?, attendees?), update_event (eventId, partial fields), delete_event (eventId), check_free_busy (start, end). Export tool definition objects for each. These tools must NOT be called by the agent directly — they are only called by the orchestrator's confirmation executor after user confirms.
acceptance: create_event, update_event, delete_event, check_free_busy tool definitions exported
All four tools included in agent's tool definitions array
Tool functions accept correct TypeScript types matching MCP contracts

tasks.title: Confirmation pattern: active_confirmation storage
tasks.description: Implement confirmation record storage using the active_confirmation JSONB column on conversation_context. Write saveConfirmation(chatId, payload: ConfirmationPayload): stores the payload keyed to the chat_id by upserting the latest row's active_confirmation column. Write loadConfirmation(chatId): returns pending ConfirmationPayload or null. Write clearConfirmation(chatId): nulls the column. Implement 10-minute expiry check on load.
acceptance: saveConfirmation upserts payload for chat_id
loadConfirmation returns null if no pending confirmation
loadConfirmation returns null if confirmation is older than 10 minutes
clearConfirmation sets column to null
Only one active confirmation per chat_id — new proposal overwrites old

tasks.title: EP-02-01/02: Create calendar event with confirmation
tasks.description: Wire the create event flow end-to-end. When user describes a meeting, agent extracts who/what/when/where/duration and responds with a structured proposal (formatted text summary). Orchestrator calls saveConfirmation with ConfirmationPayload {action: 'create_event', data: {...}}. Bot renders Confirm/Edit/Cancel inline keyboard. On Confirm callback, orchestrator calls create_event tool and sends success message. On Cancel, clears confirmation and sends cancellation message.
acceptance: Smoke test 4: 'add a meeting with Tom at 3pm Friday' → proposal with Confirm/Edit/Cancel buttons
Smoke test 5: Confirm → event created in Google Calendar
Cancel → confirmation cleared, no event created
Proposal message includes: title, date, time, duration, location (if any)

tasks.title: EP-02-03: Update calendar event with confirmation
tasks.description: Wire the update event flow. When user describes a change ('move my 3pm Friday to 4pm'), agent calls get_events_range to identify the event, then proposes an update with a before/after summary. Saves ConfirmationPayload {action: 'update_event', eventId, data: partialFields}. On Confirm, calls update_event. On Edit, prompts for further changes and proposes again.
acceptance: Smoke test 6: 'move my 3pm Friday to 4pm' → before/after proposal with confirmation buttons
Confirm → event updated in Google Calendar
Edit → agent re-prompts, user can change details, new proposal shown

tasks.title: EP-02-04: Delete calendar event with confirmation
tasks.description: Wire the delete event flow. When user asks to delete an event, agent identifies it (using get_events_range). If ambiguous (multiple matches), agent lists options and asks user to clarify before proposing. Proposal shows event summary. Saves ConfirmationPayload {action: 'delete_event', eventId}. On Confirm, calls delete_event.
acceptance: Smoke test 7: 'delete my 3pm Friday' → event summary proposal with confirmation
Confirm → event deleted from Google Calendar
Ambiguous match → agent lists matching events and asks user to specify

tasks.title: EP-02-05: Free/busy check
tasks.description: Wire the check_free_busy tool. When user asks 'am I free Thursday afternoon?', agent resolves the time range, calls check_free_busy, and returns a plain-language free/busy response. If busy, include the name of the adjacent or conflicting event. No confirmation step required (read-only operation).
acceptance: Smoke test 8: 'am I free Thursday afternoon?' → clear free/busy response
If busy, response names the conflicting event
No Confirm/Edit/Cancel buttons shown (read-only)

tasks.title: Vitest test suite scaffold and unit tests
tasks.description: Configure Vitest for all packages. Write unit tests for: middleware.ts isAllowedChat (T-06), context rolling window (T-09), confirmation expiry logic (T-16), keyboard builders (T-07), env.ts missing-var throw (T-02), migration idempotency (T-03). All tests must pass with pnpm test.
acceptance: pnpm test passes with zero failures
isAllowedChat: allowed ID returns true, any other returns false
Rolling window: 25 saves leaves exactly 20 rows
Confirmation expiry: payload older than 10 min returns null
env.ts: missing required var throws at startup

tasks.title: Railway service configuration and deployment
tasks.description: Add railway.json (or railway.toml) configuration for bot and orchestrator services. Add Dockerfile or nixpacks config for each. Confirm DATABASE_URL, TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN, TELEGRAM_ALLOWED_CHAT_ID, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, ORCHESTRATOR_URL are documented in .env.example with descriptions. Deployments triggered by push to main. Tag phase completion as phase-1-complete after Validator PASS.
acceptance: Both services deploy to Railway without build errors
Bot service can reach orchestrator via ORCHESTRATOR_URL on Railway private network
All environment variables documented in .env.example
No .env files committed to repository

smoke_tests.description: Send 'hello' to the bot — Claude responds within 8 seconds
Send a message from a different chat_id — bot does not respond
Send 'what have I got today?' — calendar events returned
Send 'add a meeting with Tom at 3pm Friday' — agent proposes event with full details, Confirm/Edit/Cancel buttons appear
Tap Confirm — event created in Google Calendar
Send 'move my 3pm Friday to 4pm' — agent proposes update, user confirms
Send 'delete my 3pm Friday' — agent proposes deletion, user confirms
Send 'am I free Thursday afternoon?' — agent checks calendar and responds

tech_stack.database: PostgreSQL 16 via pg (node-postgres), raw parameterised SQL only
